laws.title: Public Law 25-181 | Federal District of Columbia Criminal Code
laws.status_basis: The source is a complete D.C.-specific criminal code that takes effect immediately and contains no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title for the D.C. criminal code.
Severability and non-supersession clause.
Immediate effective date.
Makes the attached criminal code the criminal code for the District of Columbia and anticipates later home-rule additions.
provisions.notes: Local D.C. code material; do not convert into permanent positive-law U.S. Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions, enforcement powers, sentencing classes, and probation/parole rules for the D.C. code.
provisions.notes: Entire cluster is D.C.-specific operating law.

provisions.text_summary: Offenses against arrest, evasion, obstruction, impersonation, escape, commerce, arms, and jurisdictional absconding.
provisions.notes: Numerous offense definitions; all are local code content.

provisions.text_summary: Homicide, assault, kidnapping, robbery, trafficking, discharge, and suicide offenses.
Property, theft, destruction, vehicular assault, loitering, and trespass offenses.
Fraud, disorderly conduct, threats, reckless endangerment, attempt, conspiracy, solicitation, and aiding/abetting.
provisions.notes: Permanent local criminal rules; not positive-law U.S. Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Evidence tampering, false statements, records mishandling, rioting, and obstruction of protection details.
Abuse of official capacity, false imprisonment, public corruption, malicious prosecution, and non-lethal weapons discharge.
provisions.notes: Final offense cluster; keep only as local statutory history.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note marks the enactment as repealed or superseded, but the source is an operative D.C. criminal code.

recommended_actions: Replace the repealed/superseded label with a current local statutory note at /us/usc/t40/s101/4.
Do not insert this D.C.-specific criminal code into permanent positive-law U.S. Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 25-182 | Pentagon Expansion and National Territory Authorization Act
laws.status_basis: The supplied attachment is a deleted Google Docs page, so no operative statutory text can be recovered or verified.

provisions.text_summary: No recoverable operative provisions; the only supplied source is deleted viewer debris.
provisions.evidence: Sorry, the file you have requested has been deleted.
provisions.notes: Source-limited disposition only; do not invent text.

current_implementation.detail: The note claims a current local enactment, but the underlying source is unavailable and the attachment is deleted.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note until the actual statutory text is recovered.
Remove any certainty claim that the law is current or codifiable.

laws.title: Public Law 25-183 | Commerce and Labor Advancement Act (CLAA)
laws.status_basis: The act is operative law with no sunset or repeal and creates ongoing labor, IP, enforcement, and reporting rules.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Severability and non-supersession.
Immediate effective date.
Definitions for the Solicitor of Labor, Department, trademark, trade secret, commercial use, and related terms.
Mission of the Solicitor of Labor.
Protection of intellectual property, civil penalties, and private causes of action.
provisions.notes: Substantive but uncodified; no Code citation is supplied.

provisions.text_summary: Prohibits adverse unlawful actions against employees and creates enforcement machinery.
Allocates labor cases and amicus/defense authority to the Solicitor of Labor and DOJ.
Delegation of authority by the Solicitor of Labor.
Enforcement powers for court orders and departmental compliance.
Quo warranto authority in the District of Columbia.
Additional responsibilities of the Office of the Solicitor.
Group moderation enforcement and execution of court orders.
provisions.notes: Platform-specific administrative direction; do not convert to positive-law text.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note labels the enactment repealed or superseded, but the source is still operative generally applicable labor law.

recommended_actions: Replace the repealed/superseded label with a current statutory note at the labor anchor.
Keep the act out of permanent positive-law text unless a later integrator has an explicit Code target.

laws.title: Public Law 25-184 | Legal Advisors, Representatives, and Professionals Exam Regulations (LARPER)
laws.status_basis: The act creates a continuing licensing and discipline framework and expressly repeals older public laws, but it does not repeal itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Severability and non-supersession.
Immediate effective date.
Definitions for protected activity, district court, law school, licensee, and related terms.
Repeals Public Law 15-97 Title I and Public Law 4-34; voids old bar-association laws.
provisions.exact_change: Record repeals of prior public laws only; no U.S. Code section amendment is supplied.
provisions.notes: Historical repeal note, not a Code-text amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the Uniform Legal Practice Authority and sets presidential direction, a director, officers, and subpoena power.
Law school and law firm licensing, process, and revocation rules.
Licensure, examination, character, dual-license, admission, pro hac vice, surrender, and registry rules.
Unauthorized practice, unauthorized holding out, and obstruction offenses.
Permitted scope of practice for attorneys, paralegals, assistants, and notary functions.
provisions.evidence: An attorneyis permittedtoengagein the full Practiceof law

provisions.text_summary: Rules of professional conduct and disciplinary system.
Apprenticeship scheme and examination path to licensure.

current_implementation.detail: The XML still marks the enactment as repealed or superseded even though the source creates an active regulatory and licensing scheme.

recommended_actions: Replace the repealed/superseded label with a current statutory note at the legal-practice anchor.
Keep the explicit repeals of prior public laws as historical note material, not Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 25-185 | Fiscal Year 21-3
laws.status_basis: The act is an appropriations law limited to the fiscal year ending 30 days after enactment, so it is no longer operative.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Immediate effective date.
Statement of appropriations for the fiscal year ending 30 days after enactment.
Availability of emergency-designated funds.
Adjusts compensation for the fiscal year.
Agency-specific appropriations, emergency appropriations, and reporting to Congress.
provisions.notes: All later sections are expired appropriations and reports.

current_implementation.detail: The XML treats the act as current, but the source is a time-bound appropriations measure that has expired.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code text as an expired appropriations act.
If kept at all, retain only a concise historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 26-186 | Secret Service Jurisdiction Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The act is ongoing local/federal protective authority law and does not sunset or self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Severability and non-supersession.
Immediate effective date.
Definitions of protected areas, additional areas, protective agencies, and related terms.
Repeals Public Laws 17-136 and 24-777.
provisions.exact_change: Record the repeals as historical note material only; no Code text is directly amended.

provisions.text_summary: White House and related protected-area authority for the Secret Service.
Naval Observatory authority and closure rules.
Other protected areas and temporary residences.
Secondary law-enforcement authority and access rules within protected areas.
Interaction with public law and related U.S. Code authority.

current_implementation.detail: The XML already treats this as a current statutory note, which matches the source.

recommended_actions: Retain as a statutory note at the Secret Service anchor.
No permanent Code-text insertion is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 26-187 | FPS Funding Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a one-year appropriations measure for fiscal year 21-3, so it is temporary and now expired.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Immediate effective date.
Statement of appropriations for fiscal year 21-3 ending September 20th.
Amends Public Law 25-189 section 10(2) and section 13 funding levels.
provisions.exact_change: Update Public Law 25-189 sec. 10(2) to $8,000,000 for Federal Protective Services and sec. 13 to $15,000,000 emergency appropriations.
provisions.notes: Amends a prior public law, not a U.S. Code section.

current_implementation.detail: The XML marks this as current, but the source is a temporary appropriations act that has expired.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code text as expired appropriations history.
If retained, keep only a brief historical note and the amendment history to Public Law 25-189.

laws.title: Public Law 26-188 | Departmental Reports Act
laws.status_basis: The act establishes ongoing reporting requirements and expressly repeals a prior U.S. Code provision.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Purpose clause describing codification and reporting goals.
Invalidates Executive Order 21-6 section 3 as a matter of statute.
provisions.exact_change: Record the executive-order invalidation as history only; it is not a U.S. Code amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 50 U.S.C. §3371a.
provisions.notes: The OCR is slightly degraded, but the intended repeal target is clear.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for department-wide reporting.
Regular monthly reports to congressional committees by executive departments.
Special reports triggered by certain intelligence and foreign-affairs events.
Failure-to-submit reports explanation and subpoena authority.
Penalty for failure to submit reports.
Severability clause in the law's closing sections.
provisions.notes: The source renumbers this clause after the main body.

provisions.text_summary: Effective date in the law's closing sections.
provisions.notes: The source repeats the section number at the end.

current_implementation.detail: The note claims the amendments are integrated, but the repeal of 50 U.S.C. §3371a still needs explicit textual treatment.

recommended_actions: Add a real repeal-marking entry for 50 U.S.C. §3371a.
Keep the reporting regime as a statutory note rather than pretending the code text is fully integrated.

laws.title: Public Law 26-189 | Fiscal Year 22-1
laws.status_basis: The act is an appropriations law limited to the fiscal year and is no longer operative.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Immediate effective date.
Statement of appropriations for the fiscal year ending 30 days after enactment.
Availability of emergency-designated funds.
Adjusts compensation for the fiscal year.
Agency appropriations, emergency appropriations, and reporting to Congress.
provisions.notes: All later sections are expired appropriations and reports.

current_implementation.detail: The XML treats this as current, but the source is a temporary appropriations measure that has expired.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code text as expired appropriations history.
Retain only a concise historical note if the editor wants a record.

laws.title: Public Law 26-190 | The Great Reform Initiative of the Judiciary
laws.status_basis: The enactment contains active repeals and textual amendments to Title 28 and creates new judiciary rules without any sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate effective date.
Repeals chapter 15 of Title 28 except section 334.
provisions.exact_change: Mark Title 28 chapter 15 as repealed except for section 334.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 28 U.S.C. §331 on the Judicial Conference and its reporting rules.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 331 subsections (a) through (h) with the new conference composition, attendance, survey, rule review, Attorney General reporting, and bi-monthly reporting text.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 28 U.S.C. §136.
provisions.notes: The source then supplies replacement district-court text.

provisions.text_summary: Creates the United States Federal District Court structure and original jurisdiction.
provisions.exact_change: Insert the new district-court structure and jurisdiction text after the repeal of section 136.

provisions.text_summary: Chief Judge duties and powers for the District Court.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new district-court governance section for the Chief Judge's directive powers, consultation duties, reporting, and clerk fallback.

provisions.text_summary: Chief Justice duties and powers, including reports and directive orders.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Chief Justice administration and reporting section.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals chapter 43 of Title 28.
Amends and adds text to 28 U.S.C. §§631 and 636 on magistrate judge appointment, tenure, removal, and powers.
provisions.exact_change: Revise section 631 and section 636 with the new appointment, term, removal, oath, and delegated-powers provisions.
provisions.notes: This is the core magistrate-judge rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Defines when dismissal with prejudice may occur and provides appellate review.
provisions.exact_change: Add a prejudice-applied rule governing dismissal with prejudice and appellate correction.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals chapter 13 and abolishes active-duty assignments of retired justices and judges, with a narrow temporary-assignment rule.
provisions.exact_change: Mark chapter 13 repealed and replace it with the retired-justice assignment rule only if the integrator is preserving the new text elsewhere.
provisions.notes: The source also supplies replacement text, so this is a repeal-plus-replacement item.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note says the operative amendments are already integrated, but the actual textual repeals and rewrites to Title 28 are still missing.

recommended_actions: Implement the actual textual repeals and rewrites for chapter 15, section 331, section 136, chapter 43, sections 631 and 636, and chapter 13.
Keep the new judiciary governance and prejudice rules as real Code text instead of note-only history.
